name: Don Friedman
abstract: Donald Ernest Friedman (May 4, 1935 – June 30, 2016) was an American jazz pianist. He began playing in Los Angeles and moved to New York in 1958.

concerts.label: Clark Terry and His Big Band

works.t: Blues All Day, Blues All Night

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Clark
about_quotes.evidence: Piano players were Don Friedman for a short while, and Roger Kellaway... Don Friedman was there for a while
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Clark — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Larry Ridley
about_quotes.evidence: And had a lot of great people who came through there teaching and sharing there, from Don Friedman and Frank Foster to, oh God if I start naming names I’m liable to leave somebody out.
about_quotes.doc_title: Larry Ridley interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 10, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Ralph LaLama
about_quotes.evidence: And we have a record out together, “In a Word,” Don Friedman, Dennis Irwin, Ralph LaLama and Nicole Pasternak
about_quotes.doc_title: Ralph LaLama interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, May 23, 2010

about_quotes.speaker: Akiyoshi Toshiko
about_quotes.evidence: So I was subbing... I went there... Clark started with a D-flat blues... I had to play
about_quotes.doc_title: Akiyoshi Toshiko — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
